mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls captures labor income expansion that translates into consumption and corporate revenue; positive payroll growth predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS level of 133755.0 thousand persons available at decision date
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month PAYEMS changes are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of months, or walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index inflation exceeding 3% year-over-year signals overheating that prompts monetary tightening expectations, raising discount rates and reducing equity valuations; this predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL level of 231.751 available at decision date
failure_condition: Triggered instances with CPIAUCSL year-over-year change above 3% produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of occurrences, or the mean forward return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously rising consumer prices and falling nonfarm payrolls define a stagflationary regime that squeezes corporate margins and reduces earnings multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Periods with month-over-month CPIAUCSL increases and month-over-month PAYEMS decreases are not followed by negative 20-day returns in at least 55% of instances, or the interaction term adds no marginal explanatory power beyond the individual series (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, a month-over-month increase in the federal funds rate directly raises the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS level of 0.16 percent available at decision date
failure_condition: In months where FEDFUNDS increases during identified tightening cycles, the subsequent 5-day S&P 500 return is positive in more than 50% of cases, or the mean return is not negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales signals robust consumer spending that immediately lifts revenue expectations for S&P 500 consumer-facing firms, predicting positive forward returns over 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS level of 411591.0 million dollars available at decision date
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month RSAFS changes are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of observations, or walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production signals contraction in goods-producing sectors and weaker operating leverage, forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO level of 97.0328 available at decision date
failure_condition: Months with negative INDPRO changes produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances, or the mean forward return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Jointly falling unemployment and rising housing starts signal a durable labor-market-driven housing expansion that boosts construction employment and durable goods consumption, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 7.9 and HOUST at 872.0 available at decision date
failure_condition: Periods where UNRATE declines month-over-month and HOUST rises are followed by negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of cases, or the interaction term fails to improve explanatory power versus a simple linear sum (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 800 thousand units signal a freezing residential investment sector that drags construction and consumer durables, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST level of 872.0 thousand units available at decision date
failure_condition: Observations with HOUST below 800 are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of cases, or the mean forward return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).
